■ Road Safety Report

The Roads Here
Are Different

Wildlife collisions on Vancouver Island claim four lives and injure nearly 900 drivers a year. The headlights on your vehicle may be making it worse — and you'd never know.

Hwy 19 at dusk in November isn't like driving in a city. Deer don't use crosswalks. A 200-pound buck stepping onto the road gives you maybe half a second to react — and that half second depends entirely on how far your headlights actually reach. Oxidized lenses don't just look bad. They cut your warning distance in half, on roads where warning distance is everything.

Most drivers don't notice it happening. UV damage is gradual. One year your headlights are clear, a few years later they're yellowed and you've quietly adjusted without realizing how much worse your visibility has become. By the time it's obvious, you've been driving compromised for months. On the Island, with 529 wildlife collisions recorded on Hwy 1 and 19 alone, that's not a risk worth carrying.

40%
Oxidized headlights reduce beam reach by up to 40% — the difference between stopping in time and not stopping at all.

Simple Process

How It Works

We come to you. No drop-offs, no waiting. Done in your driveway in under an hour.

01
We Assess

Lux meter check on the spot. We show you the numbers — clarity percentage, oxidation depth, and what's actually affecting your visibility.

02
We Restore

Professional UV-cured hard coat. Not spray-on, not DIY-kit quality. The same process used by auto manufacturers — applied by a specialist.

03
We Guarantee

3-year written warranty, before-and-after documentation, and a record of your service. No local competitor offers this.

Why Island Headlight Safety

Not a detailer.
A specialist.

Every competitor on mid-Vancouver Island treats headlight restoration as a side service — something bundled into a detail package, done when time allows, with no warranty and no specialization. We don't. This is all we do.

  • 3-Year Written Warranty

    Every job backed in writing. No one locally offers this. It's not a upsell — it's the standard.

  • Mobile — We Come to You

    Driveway, workplace, or fleet lot. Campbell River to Courtenay and everywhere in between.

  • UV-Cured Hard Coat

    Professional-grade coating, not spray-on. Bonds to the lens at a molecular level. Lasts years, not months.

  • Before & After Documentation

    Every job photographed. You see the difference in numbers — not just visual impression.

Common Questions

FAQ

Our UV-cured hard coat is backed by a 3-year written warranty — which means it's guaranteed to last at least that long with normal use. DIY kits typically last 3–8 months before yellowing returns. We use professional-grade materials that bond to the lens surface properly.
Almost certainly. We work on all makes and models — cars, trucks, SUVs, vans, and commercial vehicles. If the plastic lens is oxidized or yellowed, we can restore it. We've worked on everything from 2005 Corollas to fleet pickup trucks.
DIY kits sand the lens and apply a thin sealant that wears off quickly — usually within months. Without a UV-cured hard coat applied properly, the oxidation returns fast. We've seen vehicles that were "restored" with kits 4 months ago come in looking worse than before. The results are not the same.
We come to you. Driveway, workplace parking lot, fleet yard — wherever your vehicle is parked. No drop-offs, no waiting for your car back. Most jobs are done in 30–60 minutes on site.
Text us a photo of your headlights at (250) 555-0123. We'll send back a quote within minutes. No phone tag, no appointment needed just to find out the price.
